Shadow Slave by Guiltythree

Continue Chapters list
  • Information
  • Last chapters
Growing up in poverty, Sunny never expected anything good from life. However, even he did not... Read more
Growing up in poverty, Sunny never expected anything good from life. However, even he did not anticipate being chosen by the Nightmare Spell and becoming one of the Awakened — an elite group of people gifted with supernatural powers.

Transported into a ruined magical world, he found himself facing against terrible monsters — and other Awakened — in a deadly battle of survival.

What’s worse, the shadow powers he received happened to possess a small, but potentially fatal side effect… Collapse
  • Status in COO: Ongoing

      1. Offline

        + 1 ⋅ 0 -

        1. Offline
          Shiro Ippikikokami
          Shiro Ippikikokami 15 Mar 2026, 15:29 ➥ MTL Ancestor
          + 2 ⋅ 1 -
          Shadow Slave is genuinely... Good. Not "good for a web novel" — Good.

          It has a protagonist with psychological depth, a world with depth and architecture, and this is my personal fav: a tonal control that most authors in this space never achieve, and enough genuine feeling in its best moments to stick with you

          I'll be honest with the flaws too: length, repetition, occasional narrative sag. But the foundation is strong enough, I think, that you'll find yourself not minding them much, or powering through them.

          If you're already reading it, you're in good hands. If you're deciding whether to start — yes, start. The first volume alone is worth it.
          Read more
      2. Offline
        Shiro Ippikikokami
        Shiro Ippikikokami 15 Mar 2026, 15:26 ➥ Vsk
        + 1 ⋅ 1 -
        imho, Shadow Slave is genuinely good. Not just... "good for a web novel" — it's Good. With a capital G. I repeated it three times coz it's important.
        A protagonist with psychological depth, a world with real depth and good intellectual architecture(the best I've seen is LoTM but who compares it to that? that thing is a beast), tonal control that most authors never achieve(it's genuinely hard, trust me), and enough genuine feeling in its best moments to stick with you.

        I'll be fair and list its flaws: length, repetition, occasional narrative sag. But the foundation is strong enough that you'll want to stick with it (i said 'want', not that you 'will', because exhaustion is a real thing for WNs with millions of words and thousands of chapters...)

        If you're already reading it, you're in good hands. If you're deciding whether to start — yes, start. The first volume alone is worth it.
        Read more
      3. Online Offline
